taskDescription: Drag the correct embryological terms into the blanks to complete statements about the second week of development.
textField:
The blastocyst begins *implantation* around Day 6-7 post-fertilisation. The normal site of implantation is the *posterior* wall of the *fundus* of the uterus. The implantation window corresponds to Days *20-24* of the menstrual cycle, when the endometrium is in the *secretory* phase and maximally receptive.

The trophoblast differentiates into an inner *cytotrophoblast* (cellular layer that is mitotically active) and an outer *syncytiotrophoblast* (multinucleated, invasive layer that erodes into the endometrium). The syncytiotrophoblast produces *hCG* (human chorionic gonadotropin), which is the basis of pregnancy tests and maintains the *corpus* luteum.

The inner cell mass (embryoblast) differentiates into the bilaminar embryonic disc consisting of two layers: the *epiblast* (dorsal, columnar) and the *hypoblast* (ventral, cuboidal). Two cavities form during the second week: the *amniotic* cavity (above the epiblast) and the primary *yolk* sac (below the hypoblast).

Ectopic implantation most commonly occurs in the *ampulla* of the uterine tube. The extraembryonic *mesoderm* splits to form the chorionic cavity (extraembryonic coelom).

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the events of the second week of development.
Good effort! Revise implantation and bilaminar disc formation.
Excellent understanding of second week embryology!
